set_fields.description: Starting a Ralph loop should pin the complete Tau-owned concrete execution contract for future controller and child sessions. The loop should not later infer tools, model/thinking, sandbox, or agent availability from the active Pi session unless the user explicitly recaptures configuration.
set_fields.design: During /ralph start, capture the current concrete execution contract (model, thinking, execution policy), sandbox profile, active tool names via pi.getActiveTools(), available tool metadata via pi.getAllTools(), and enabled agents from the resolved AgentRegistry plus current non-Ralph project/session agent selection. Persist this as the Ralph capability/execution contract in .pi/loops/state/<task-id>.json. Treat resume as using the pinned contract rather than refreshing from ambient state. Include a clear error path for missing active tools or unknown enabled agents.
set_fields.acceptance_criteria: New Ralph loop state includes a pinned concrete model/thinking execution contract, sandbox profile, active tool policy, and enabled agent list; /ralph resume does not silently refresh the contract from ambient Pi state; tests prove that changing Pi active tools or selected model after start does not change the persisted Ralph contract.
